diagrams.title: Quantifier Negation Rules
diagrams.description: Shows how negation transforms universal and existential quantifiers
elements: Arrow pointing from negated universal to existential with negated predicate
Arrow from negated existential to universal with negated predicate
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the transformation rules for negating quantified statements

diagrams.title: Inference Rules Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between universal and existential instantiation and generalization
elements: Universal Instantiation (top left) → arrow down to P(c)
Existential Generalization (bottom left) → arrow up to ∃x P(x)
Universal Generalization (bottom right) → arrow up to ∀x P(x)
Existential Instantiation (top right) → arrow down to P(c)
Shows how each rule moves between quantified and non-quantified forms
diagrams.purpose: Understand how to move between general and specific statements in proofs
